source: Ian Lewis (Mads Tech), House Select Committee, independent security researchers
detail: PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise. Board part numbers match. Controller RRC01 is relabeled DJI RC Pro with identical firmware. Camera module, gimbal, propulsion all DJI-sourced. DJI P1 OcuSync chipset present — proprietary ASIC that cannot be independently sourced.

source: Andreas (@Bin4ryDigit), Jon Sawyer, jcase, Konrad Iturbe
detail: Firmware signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys — DJI retains root key control. Built on DJI SDK. SecNeo/BangCle Chinese security framework present — not confirmed decrypted. MAVProxyUser documented firmware accessible on DJI's own CDN, contradicting independent hosting claims.

Anzu did NOT disclose DJI relationship in initial FCC filings (Dec 2023). Only disclosed after security researchers publicly revealed connection in mid-2024. House Committee specifically cited this omission.

source: White Knight Labs pentest (published by Aloft), Texas AG investigation
detail: White Knight Labs pentest confirmed data goes exclusively to Aloft servers — but found references to Chinese domains within system (remediated by Aloft). Texas AG alleges Aloft itself is a DJI-integrated platform. GitHub repo documented firmware accessible on DJI CDN.

Warnas in YouTube interview: DJI execs asked 'what can we do?' about losing market share. Told NYT arrangement was 'essentially DJI's idea.' Told Washington Post about per-unit payments: 'I like to be unaware of that.' House Committee: CEO admitted primary purpose was to circumvent legislative restrictions.

source: CEO official EOL notice via sUAS News
detail: Raptor-series discontinued due to component shortages. NDAA 2025 demand surge Nov/Dec depleted stock. ~50% components from China. DJI P1 OcuSync chipset — proprietary ASIC — likely insurmountable bottleneck. CEO: 'We are far from done.'

XTI Aerospace acquired 100% of Anzu for $3.175M ($1.44M cash + $954K note + 521,966 Class B units). Concurrent: Unusual Machines invested $25M in XTI preferred. Low price reflected limited standalone valuation.

source: Anzu website, dealer network, agency purchase records
detail: Marketed to public safety at $5,099-7,699 — filling 50-70% price gap between DJI ($3,500-6,500) and Blue UAS ($15,000-25,000+). Sold through 7+ dealers to police departments in AR, IA, and likely others (ParaZero SafeAir Raptor system suggests broader adoption). No geofencing — removed DJI's restrictions.

Specta Air has identical PCBs to DJI Air 3. Same DJI P1 chipset. RF emission reports differ only by date and logo removal. Firmware forked from DJI with branding stripped. No independent technology whatsoever.

Sold primarily through Amazon with zero corporate transparency. No acknowledgment of DJI relationship. No cybersecurity disclosure. No country of origin on product listings. House Committee called it 'a second ongoing attempt by DJI to white label its products.'

SkyRover app connects DIRECTLY to DJI cloud infrastructure. Jon Sawyer found identical encryption keys. Users can log in using existing DJI credentials. This is the most direct DJI connection of any white-label — no Aloft-style intermediary.

Zero corporate transparency — no ownership, no HQ, no country of origin on website. Operated through SZ Knowact Robot Technology (Shenzhen). Researchers documented layered structure: 'Knowact gets banned, rotate a new OEM, SkyRover brand is intact.' Part of network of 9+ suspected DJI shells (Skyany, Jovistar, Fikaxo, Lyno Dynamics, Spatial Hover — last one's FCC docs contained DJI logos).

Four regulatory actions in 18 months: Commerce Entity List (July 2024, shipping to Russia), DoD Section 1260H Chinese Military Companies (Jan 2025), NDAA Section 1709 (Dec 2025, named alongside DJI), FCC Covered List (Dec 22, 2025).

source: Mulvenon research report, Chinese corporate filings
detail: Chinese government 'Little Giant' designation. Seed investors include CITIC Corporation subsidiary (ultimately owned by China's Ministry of Finance). Chinese-language website markets to 'public security, army, national defense, and armed police.' Founded by Li Hongjing who also founded Shenzhen Daotong Intelligent Aviation.
